actions.title: Public GitHub Repository
actions.approval_phrase: Approve public GitHub publication for the clean agentops-control-tower candidate.
actions.purpose: Satisfy the public open-source repository requirement and give judges a runnable code package.
actions.exact_operation: Run scripts/verify_public_repo_publication_gate.py with the exact approval phrase, public git identity, and manual safety confirmations, then use scripts/publish_public_repo_after_approval.py to create and push the public repository only after explicit approval.
actions.after_approval: Run the public repository publication preflight gate with the exact approval phrase, public git identity, and manual safety confirmations, then create and push the public repository through the guarded helper and record the public repository URL.
actions.main_risk: Publishing the wrong folder or initializing Git inside the private workspace could expose private workspace material, local paths, or non-public artifacts.
actions.verification: Review reports/latest_public_repo_publication_preflight.html, open the public repository URL, rerun the public candidate scans and isolated staging dry run, then validate the URL before writing it locally.

actions.title: Public Demo Video
actions.approval_phrase: Approve recording and public upload of the Agentic Incident Command Center demo video.
actions.purpose: Give judges a fast walkthrough of the dashboard, local SPL proof, approval queue, and MCP Remediation Ledger.
actions.exact_operation: Run scripts/verify_public_video_upload_gate.py with the exact approval phrase and manual safety confirmations, record the browser walkthrough using reports/latest_demo_tour.html and submission/VIDEO_RECORDING_RUNBOOK.md, then upload publicly only after explicit approval.
actions.after_approval: Run the public video upload preflight gate with the exact approval phrase and manual safety confirmations, record, review, and upload the public demo video, then record the public video URL.
actions.main_risk: A recording could expose private tabs, local paths, accounts, credentials, or overstate live Splunk MCP proof.
actions.verification: Review reports/latest_public_video_upload_preflight.html, watch the uploaded public video end to end, confirm it is under 3 minutes and screen-safe, then validate the URL.

actions.title: Optional Live Splunk / MCP Proof
actions.approval_phrase: Approve optional live Splunk and Splunk MCP proof using synthetic data only.
actions.purpose: Strengthen the Best Use of Splunk MCP Server bonus claim if credentials, time, and scope are acceptable.
actions.exact_operation: Set up Splunk account/license, import only synthetic data into agentops_events, configure Splunk MCP Server with approved scope, and capture proof.
actions.after_approval: Configure Splunk/MCP with approved scope, import only synthetic data, capture proof, and rerun claim validation.
actions.main_risk: Requires account setup, credentials, possible cost, and careful scope control.
actions.verification: Use the prompt pack and proof capture manifest, confirm SPL queries and MCP-assisted answer cite synthetic event IDs, then update wording only after proof exists.

actions.title: Approved URL Writeback
actions.approval_phrase: Approve writing the verified public repository and demo video URLs into local submission artifacts.
actions.purpose: Replace pending URL placeholders only after both public URLs are real and approved.
actions.exact_operation: Run prepare_submission_urls.py with both approved URLs and --write-approved, then rebuild final copy.
actions.after_approval: Run prepare_submission_urls.py with both approved URLs and --write-approved, then rebuild final copy.
actions.main_risk: Writing unverified or private URLs could make the Devpost packet look ready when it is not.
actions.verification: Rerun URL validation and confirm final_submit_ready changes only when both public URLs pass.

actions.title: Devpost Final Submission
actions.approval_phrase: Approve final Devpost submission for Agentic Incident Command Center.
actions.purpose: Complete the hackathon submission after all required public artifacts and claim boundaries are verified.
actions.exact_operation: After public repo and public video URLs are inserted and validation passes, review reports/latest_devpost_final_copy.md and press the Devpost submit button only with explicit final approval.
actions.after_approval: Use the Devpost command plan, fill fields, perform human readback, then press submit only after final approval.
actions.main_risk: Submitting with pending URLs, wrong track, missing architecture evidence, or unverified live Splunk claims.
actions.verification: Read back the submitted Devpost page/status and save local post-submit evidence.

human_confirmations.confirmation_needed: Entrant is at least the age of majority where they reside and is not resident/domiciled in an excluded jurisdiction.
human_confirmations.why_codex_cannot_confirm: This depends on personal/legal facts and should be confirmed by the user before Devpost submission.

human_confirmations.confirmation_needed: Entrant is not a judge, promotion entity, government/state-owned entity, excluded affiliate, household member, or otherwise conflicted participant.
human_confirmations.why_codex_cannot_confirm: This depends on employment, affiliation, household, and conflict facts outside the local packet.

human_confirmations.confirmation_needed: If submitted as a team or organization, the submitter is authorized to act as the representative and the team size is within the rules.
human_confirmations.why_codex_cannot_confirm: The local packet does not know whether the Devpost entry will be individual, team, or organization.

human_confirmations.confirmation_needed: The entrant owns the submitted work or has permission for every included component, and no third-party rights are violated.
human_confirmations.why_codex_cannot_confirm: The package can audit files and licenses, but final ownership representation belongs to the entrant.

human_confirmations.confirmation_needed: The project was not developed with prohibited financial or preferential support from Sponsor or Administrator.
human_confirmations.why_codex_cannot_confirm: This depends on external business, employment, funding, and contract facts.

boundary: This packet is local approval guidance only. It does not publish, upload, submit, create accounts, configure credentials, write approved URLs, or update Devpost.
recommended_next_step: Ask for public GitHub and public demo video approval first. Keep URL writeback and Devpost final submission blocked until both public URLs are verified.
